serviceDescription: The Windows Server and SQL Server on Amazon EC2 calculator provides a pricing estimate for specific workloads. It recommends suitable cloud deployment options and cost-saving pricing models based on licensing and infrastructure inputs.

templates.machineSpecificationDescription: Configure machine specifications for each of your machines.
templates.pricingStrategyDescription: Select a pricing model and review cost calculations.
templates.licensingTenancyLabel: Determine your licensing and tenancy recommendation
templates.licensingTenancyDescription: Specify your licensing scenario to determine your tenancy qualifications. The tenancy determines if compute resources are shared or in physical isolation in AWS.
templates.emptyStateAlert: At least one machine specification is required

cards.title: Licensing and tenancy recommendation
cards.helpText:
Shared tenancy is a default tenancy mode most commonly used in AWS. Shared tenancy is when multiple Amazon EC2 instances from different customers reside on the same piece of physical hardware. AWS determines which host the instances run on.

By using license-included instances, you can access fully compliant Microsoft software licenses bundled with Amazon EC2. Buying from AWS with Microsoft included licensing included has the following benefits:

  • AWS manages licensing compliance.
  • AWS supports current and various legacy versions of Microsoft software.
  • There are no software upgrade fees.

Dedicated Host is a physical server with Amazon EC2 instance capacity fully dedicated to your use. You can use dedicated hosts to use your existing per-socket, per-core, or per-VM software licenses, including Windows Server, Microsoft SQL Server, SUSE, and Linux Enterprise Server. Other customers will not use the same piece of hardware as you. If you’ve already purchased Microsoft software, you can bring your own licenses (BYOL) to the AWS Cloud (subject to Microsoft license terms). You also have the following benefits:

  • Take advantage of the efficiencies of the cloud while using your existing perpetual licenses.
  • Extend the lifecycle of your software without additional hardware costs.
  • Expedite your migration to the cloud by using existing virtual machine images.
The use of Microsoft software is subject to Microsoft's terms. You are responsible for complying with Microsoft licensing.

footer.text: You may save costs by bringing your own licenses (BYOL).
footer.helpText: BYOL is the process of bringing previously purchased, on-premises licenses to AWS. You may be able to take advantage of your existing Microsoft volume licensing investments and move to the AWS Cloud without paying additional Microsoft licensing fees.

options.title: Shared Tenancy
options.helpText:
Shared tenancy is a default tenancy mode most commonly used in AWS. Shared tenancy is when multiple EC2 instances from different customers reside on the same piece of physical hardware. AWS determines which host the instances run on.

By using license-included instances, you can access fully compliant Microsoft software licenses bundled with Amazon EC2. Buying from AWS with Microsoft included licensing included has the following benefits:

  • AWS manages licensing compliance.
  • AWS supports current and various legacy versions of Microsoft software.
  • There are no software upgrade fees.

options.content: Based on your selections, all costs will be calculated as AWS license included on Amazon EC2 shared tenancy. While you could bring your Windows Server licenses to EC2 Dedicated Hosts, you cannot bring SQL Server licenses purchased after October 1, 2019 without software assurance. SQL Server license included is not available on Dedicated Hosts.

elements.content: Fill in the template in the format specified
Upload the Excel file for your bulk estimation

components.title: Machine specifications
components.helpText:
This shows all the machine specifications from your uploaded file.


Decline status indicates that the content you uploaded has unsupported values. You can update the value and re-upload the file. If you proceed without fixing the values, the corresponding machine specification row(s) are not included in the estimate.
components.linkContext: Generating Windows Server and SQL Server estimates on Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts

components.title: Review Dedicated Hosts
components.helpText:
This shows all of the recommended Dedicated Hosts based on your machine specifications.


The Dedicated Hosts recommendation is automatically calculated based on the vCPU and memory.


Unused vCPUs
if unused vCPUs has less than 50%, we recommend you to find alternates such as using Share Tenancy for those instances.


License details
View licenses related to your machine specifications and Dedicated Hosts.
components.linkContext: Generating Windows Server and SQL Server estimates on Amazon EC2 Dedicated Hosts

cards.title: Pricing strategy
cards.description: Choose your pricing strategy. Your choices are reflected in the cost at the bottom of the page
cards.helpText:
Amazon EC2 Instance Savings Plans provide further savings but require a commitment of individual EC2 instance families in an AWS Region. This plan provides savings on the selected instance family in the selected AWS Region regardless of AZ, size, OS, or tenancy.

Compute Savings Plans provide provide the most flexibility, and automatically apply to EC2 instance usage regardless of instance family, size, AZ, AWS Region, OS, or tenancy. This plan also applies to Fargate usage.

Standard Reserved Instances provide you discounts of up to 75% compared to On-Demand EC2 instance pricing, and can be purchased for a 1-year or 3-year term. You have the flexibility to change the Availability Zone, the instance size, and networking type of Standard Reserved instances.

On-Demand instances allows you to pay for compute capacity by the hour or by the second (maximum of 60 seconds) with no long-term commitment. This frees you from the costs and complexities of planning, purchasing, and maintaining hardware and transforms what are commonly large fixed costs into much smaller variable costs.

components.label: Storage volumes per specification
components.title: Storage volumes
components.helpText:
io2 is the latest generation of the Provisioned IOPS SSD volumes that is designed to provide 100X durability of 99.999% as well as a 10X higher IOPS to storage ratio of 500 IOPS for every provisioned GB –at the same price as the previous generation (io1). io2 is a high performance EBS storage option designed for business-critical, I/O intensive database applications, such as SAP HANA, Oracle, Microsoft SQL Server, and IBM DB2 that have high durability requirements. It is designed to deliver a consistent baseline performance of up to 500 IOPS/GB to a maximum of 64,000 IOPS. io2 volumes also provide up to 1,000 MB/s of throughput per volume. To maximize the benefit of io2, we recommend using EBS-optimized EC2 instances. When attached to EBS-optimized EC2 instances, io2 is designed to achieve single-digit millisecond latencies and is designed to deliver the provisioned performance 99.9% of the time.

io1 is backed by solid-state drives (SSDs) and is a high performance EBS storage option designed for critical, I/O intensive database and application workloads, as well as throughput-intensive database and data warehouse workloads, such as HBase, Vertica, and Cassandra. These volumes are ideal for both IOPS-intensive and throughput-intensive workloads that require low latency and have moderate durability requirements or include built-in application redundancy.

gp3 volumes are the latest generation of general-purpose SSD-based EBS volumes that enable customers to provision performance independent of storage capacity, while providing up to 20% lower pricing per GB than existing gp2 volumes. The new gp3 volumes deliver a baseline performance of 3,000 IOPS and 125 MiBps at any volume size. Customers looking for higher performance can scale up to 16,000 IOPS and 1,000 MiBps for an additional fee. gp3 volumes are designed to offer single-digit millisecond latency while delivering the provisioned performance 99% of the time, making them ideal for a wide variety of applications that require high performance at low cost, including virtual desktops, medium sized single instance databases such as Microsoft SQL Server, Cassandra, MySQL, and Oracle DB, Hadoop analytics clusters, low-latency interactive apps, dev & test, and boot volumes.

gp2 is the default EBS volume type for Amazon EC2 instances. These volumes are supported by solid-state drives (SSDs), and suitable for a broad range of transactional workloads This includes dev or test environments, low-latency interactive applications, and boot volumes. Gp2 is designed to offer single-digit millisecond latency, deliver a consistent baseline performance of 3 IOPS/GB (minimum 100 IOPS) to a maximum of 16,000 IOPS, and provide up to 250 MB/s of throughput per volume.

components.fieldDescription: Select an Amazon EC2 instance type. You can choose to obtain a recommended instance type or search for a specific Amazon EC2 instance.

context.helpText: Amazon EC2 provides a wide selection of instance types optimized to fit different use cases. Instance types comprise varying combinations of CPU, memory, storage, and networking capacity and give you the flexibility to choose the appropriate mix of resources for your applications. Each instance type includes one or more instance sizes, allowing you to scale your resources to the requirements of your target workload.

Amazon EBS–optimized instances
An Amazon EBS–optimized instance uses an optimized configuration stack and provides additional, dedicated capacity for Amazon EBS I/O. This optimization provides the best performance for your EBS volumes by minimizing contention between Amazon EBS I/O and other traffic from your instance.

radioOptions.label: Obtain an Amazon EC2 instance type recommendation
radioOptions.description: Enter machine details, and Windows Server and SQL Server on Amazon EC2 calculator will recommend the lowest cost instance type.

radioOptions.label: Search for an Amazon EC2 instance type
radioOptions.description: Search all available Amazon EC2 instances.

components.label: Number of vCPUs
components.title: vCPUs
components.helpText: Amazon EC2 instances support multithreading, which enables multiple threads to run concurrently on a single CPU core. Each thread is represented as a virtual CPU (vCPU) on the instance. An instance has a default number of CPU cores, which varies according to instance type.
components.description: Enter the number of virtual machine CPUs for your machine.

components.label: Memory (GiB)
components.placeholder: Enter the number of Memory in gibibytes
components.description: Enter your memory size requirement to find the lowest cost instance for your needs.

components.label: Optimize vCPUs
components.description: Choose a custom number of vCPUs to save on vCPU-based licensing costs. For SQL Server, at least  4 vCPUs are required.
components.title: Optimize vCPUs
components.helpText:
Optimize CPUs for Amazon EC2 instance feature provides greater control of your EC2 instances on two fronts. First, you can specify a custom number of vCPUs when launching new instances to save on vCPU-based licensing costs. Second, you can disable Intel Hyper-Threading Technology (Intel HT Technology) for workloads that perform well with single-threaded CPUs, like certain high-performance computing (HPC) applications.

If you’re running database workloads like SQL Server on EC2, you want high memory, storage, and I/O bandwidth but do not require a high vCPU count since these workloads are rarely compute-bound. Memory-optimized instance families such as Amazon EC2 X1 instances and Amazon EC2 R4 instances offer high RAM-to-vCPU ratio to help you select appropriately sized Amazon EC2 instances. With Optimize CPUs, you have the added flexibility of specifying a custom number of vCPUs for new instances, while enjoying the same memory, storage, and bandwidth of a full-sized instance.

This feature enables Bring Your Own license (BYOL) customers to optimize their vCPU-based licensing costs. Please note that CPU optimized instances will have the same price as full-sized Amazon EC2 instances of the same size.

components.label: Quantity
components.description: Enter the number of Amazon EC2 instances that you need.

components.label: Number of passive instances
components.description: Enter the number of instances that are passive.
components.alertMessage: Number of passive instances cannot be more than the quantity.
components.title: SQL Passive Node
components.helpText:
A cluster includes two or more SQL servers called nodes. Nodes usually have identical configuration. One is identified as the active node, where a SQL Server instance is running the production workload. The node where the SQL Server is installed but not running known as a passive node.

Customers bringing SQL Server 2014 and later versions to AWS with Software Assurance are not required to license SQL Server on passive node.

cards.title: Pricing strategy
cards.helpText:
Amazon EC2 Instance Savings Plans provide deeper savings, in exchange for a commitment to usage of individual instance families in a region. This Savings Plans automatically provides you with savings on the selected instance family in the selected AWS Region regardless of AZ, size, OS or tenancy.

Compute Savings Plans provide the most flexibility and automatically apply to Amazon EC2 instance usage regardless of instance family, size, AZ, region, OS or tenancy. Compute Savings Plans also applies to Fargate usage.

Standard Reserved Instances provide you with a significant discount (up to 75%) compared to On-Demand instance pricing, and can be purchased for a 1-year or 3-year term. Customers have the flexibility to change the Availability Zone, the instance size, and networking type of their Standard Reserved Instances.

Purchase Convertible Reserved Instances if you need additional flexibility, such as the ability to use different instance families, operating systems, or tenancies over the Reserved Instance term. Convertible Reserved Instances provide you with a significant discount (up to 54%) compared to On-Demand Instances and can be purchased for a 1-year or 3-year term.

On-Demand instances let you pay for compute capacity by the hour or second (minimum of 60 seconds) with no long-term commitments. This frees you from the costs and complexities of planning, purchasing, and maintaining hardware and transforms what are commonly large fixed costs into much smaller variable costs.

options.label: Amazon EC2 Instance Savings Plans (Save up to 72%)

context.title: AWS Optimization and Licensing Assessment
body: Take advantage of a free program, AWS Optimization and Licensing Assessment (AWS OLA), to save on third party licensing costs and run your resources more efficiently.

context.title: End-of-Support Migration Program
body: AWS helps customers migrate their legacy Windows Server applications to the latest, supported versions of Windows Server on AWS, without any code changes. AWS offers the End-of-Support Migration Program (EMP) for Windows Server.

context.title: AWS Migration Acceleration Program
body: The AWS Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) for Windows is designed to help organizations reach their migration goals faster with AWS services, best practices, tools, and incentives.
